pages.text: The $(flesh)Bio-Forge$() is the workbench of the craft — the machine that grows every other machine, tool, weapon, and flesh block. If biomancy has a centre, this is it.
pages.title: The Bio-Forge

pages.text: Two $(italic)claws$() over a frame of raw meat, a $(italic)slime ball$() for binding, and a $(italic)Living Flesh$() core. Built at an ordinary crafting bench — the one biomancy machine that does not need a Bio-Forge to make.

$(bold)Using it.$() Open the Bio-Forge and you will find its catalogue sorted into tabs — $(italic)Machines, Tools, Components, Blocks, Misc$(). Pick a result, supply the listed biomancy parts, and the Forge grows it.$(br2)Each recipe also has a $(flesh)Nutrient cost$(). Small items cost a single unit; the $(l:5_arms/weapons)living weapons$() and $(l:5_arms/acolyte)Acolyte Armor$() cost 250 apiece. Keep the Forge's Nutrient reserve fed.
pages.title: The Catalogue

pages.text: $(bold)What it makes.$() Nearly everything. The other three machines, all flesh building blocks, membranes, the storage and utility blocks, every weapon and armour piece, the Bio-Injector and Essence Extractor — all are grown here. The recipe viewer lists each with its part cost.
pages.title: Its Reach
